body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:slash-command

## Scenario

synchronous-slash-command: a ready session invokes a slash command that responds locally WITHOUT calling the model; the session stays ready throughout the slash-command window with no -> working transition recorded for the slash command itself (scenario-meanings User-observable signal). The whole observable is the ABSENCE of a second working transition for the local command. Distinct from context-compaction (/compact DOES call the model) and session-reset (/new ends the session).

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode's interactive TUI ships in-process slash commands that respond without an LLM round-trip (/help, /sessions, /models, and the command palette entries the bundled binary carries). So in a live TUI the user CAN invoke a no-LLM local command and the conversation stays put. The capability — slash commands that resolve in-session without a model call — exists in the agent (per opencode docs / the bundled binary at /Users/ingo/.opencode/bin/opencode).

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store via ComputeMetrics keyed on the ses_… session id. A local slash that does NOT call the model writes no new assistant turn to the store, so the daemon would simply see no activity and the session would stay ready — exactly the read-from-store no-op the basic-turn / multi-turn opencode cells already prove the daemon surfaces (state stays ready when the store row is unchanged). The daemon is NOT the blocker; the observable is the absence of a working transition, which the store-read path naturally produces.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: slash-command)

The opencode interactive driver (.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh) drives opencode through BLOCKING headless `opencode run` subprocesses and implements ONLY three step types in its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch (lines 132-159): `send` (launches an `opencode run` subprocess and WAITS for it to complete), `wait_turn` (a no-op — `opencode run` already blocks until the turn ends), and `sleep`. There is NO step that delivers an in-TUI slash command against a live, held-open session.

The core blocker, proven this sweep:

**Headless `opencode run` ignores in-TUI slash commands.** A `send`-shaped recipe that sends a slash command (e.g. `/help`) LINTS in-grammar but is a semantic no-op. CONFIRMED THIS SWEEP (matching the session-reset / checkpoint-rewind probe of 2026-05-26): headless `opencode run` does NOT process in-TUI slash commands — it stores them as literal user messages, proven with inline `/model` AND `/new`. A slash command resolving SYNCHRONOUSLY in-session is, by definition, exactly the primitive headless `opencode run` cannot deliver: `opencode run "/help"` would store the literal text `/help` as a normal user message and run a model turn (driving ready -> working -> ready), which is the precise OPPOSITE of this scenario's premise (state stays ready, NO working transition). Slash commands are a live-TUI primitive, not a headless-`run` primitive.

### Not a false gap (and not caught by recipe-lint)

This is NOT the inline-argument false gap (a /model <id> or /compact that is sendable via a `slash` step): the opencode driver has NO `slash` step at all, and even if it did, headless `opencode run` would treat the slash text as a prompt and run a turn (proven this sweep), inverting the observable. The recipe is left `applicable:false` with no `script`, so there is no step-TYPE for recipe-lint.sh to reject — the gap is the absence of a way to deliver a synchronous in-TUI slash command against a live held-open session. This mirrors the opencode session-reset gap:slash-command, checkpoint-rewind gap:slash-command, user-esc-interrupt gap:interrupt, and model-switch-midsession gap:model-select cells, which recipe-lint likewise could not catch.

## What would unblock it

Replace or augment drive-opencode-interactive.sh with a LIVE-REPL driver (analogous to the tmux-based claudecode/pi/aider drivers): hold the opencode TUI open, run a warm-up turn, then deliver a no-LLM slash command (/help) to the LIVE session and dwell — so the daemon can observe the session staying ready (no working transition) across the slash window on the same ses_…. Once the driver can deliver a synchronous slash command to a live session, the cell becomes recordable; the daemon's store-read no-op (no new assistant turn -> stays ready) is the observable to verify against the recording.

## Matrix drift

The coverage matrix currently records opencode/synchronous-slash-command as agent_supports:yes, daemon_capability:unknown, driver_capability:ready (recording deferred). The driver_capability:ready is STALE — the opencode driver has no in-TUI slash-command delivery and headless `opencode run` stores slash text as a literal user prompt. The coverage cell should be updated to driver_capability:gap:slash-command (daemon_capability remains full per the ProcessOwnedStore read-from-store no-op reasoning above).

## Confidence

0.85: the driver grammar (`send|wait_turn|sleep`, blocking `opencode run` per send, no slash step) is read directly from the live driver source; the headless-slash no-op is the CONFIRMED sweep finding (inline /model and /new both stored as literal user text) and a synchronous in-session slash is the same class; opencode's no-LLM slash commands are per docs / the bundled binary. Not higher only because the unblocked recording (a live /help keeping the same ses_… session continuously ready with no working transition) has not been captured — that is the developer task this gap defers.
caveats: Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode the agent ships no-LLM in-TUI slash commands (/help etc.) and the daemon reads session state from the SQLite store (no new assistant turn -> the session stays ready, the same read-from-store no-op basic-turn / multi-turn already prove). The blocker is purely the driver's missing in-TUI slash-command delivery (gap:slash-command).

CONFIRMED THIS SWEEP (matching the 2026-05-26 session-reset / checkpoint-rewind probe): headless `opencode run` does NOT process in-TUI slash commands — it stores them as literal user messages (proven with inline /model AND /new). A synchronous in-session slash is exactly the primitive headless `opencode run` cannot deliver; `opencode run "/help"` would run a model turn (ready->working->ready), inverting this scenario's premise (state stays ready, no working transition).

Not the inline-argument false gap: the opencode driver has NO `slash` step (only send|wait_turn|sleep), and headless `opencode run` would treat the slash text as a prompt anyway. recipe-lint.sh does NOT catch this gap (the recipe is left applicable:false with no script, so there is no step-TYPE to reject) — same shape as the opencode session-reset / checkpoint-rewind gap:slash-command, user-esc-interrupt gap:interrupt, and model-switch-midsession gap:model-select cells.

sources.note: case "$TYPE" dispatch (lines 132-159) handles only send|wait_turn|sleep; each `send` launches a BLOCKING `opencode run` subprocess; no step delivers an in-TUI slash command against a live held-open session

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session state from SQLite via ComputeMetrics — a no-LLM slash writes no new assistant turn, so the session stays ready (read-from-store no-op); daemon is not the blocker
sibling opencode gap:slash-command cell — same root cause: headless `opencode run` stores in-TUI slash commands (/new, /model) as literal user text; needs a live-REPL driver
sibling opencode gap:slash-command cell — CONFIRMED this sweep that headless `opencode run` does not process in-TUI slash commands (/undo, /redo, /new, /model stored as literal user text)
anchor: the observable shape — a warm-up turn, then the session stays ready across the slash window with NO state_transition to working/waiting (the local command drives no model call)
opencode's TUI ships no-LLM in-process slash commands (/help and command-palette entries) — so agent_supports:yes for a slash command that resolves in-session without a model call
coverage cell currently driver_capability:ready (STALE) — should update to gap:slash-command; daemon_capability remains full per the ProcessOwnedStore read-from-store no-op
